event: NDAA-compliant antenna supply becomes critical bottleneck as DFR replacement mandates accelerate — 207/394 antennas in DB are US-origin but not scaled for replacement demand
evidence_basis: Existing flag: 'Idaho NASAO benchmark: $15K DJI drone → $42K compliant replacement (2.8× premium).' Parts DB: 394 antennas tracked, USA 207, China 160. New context: FCC firmware cliff in Jan 2027 (existing prediction p=0.786) means ~18,000 DJI drones in US public safety become non-compliant simultaneously. Replacement demand for compliant DFR platforms (Skydio X10D, BRINC, Parrot) will spike in Q3-Q4 2026 as agencies pre-purchase.
probability_basis: Validates existing DFR cost premium flag and adds timing precision from FCC cliff prediction. 207 US antenna options sounds sufficient but existing flag notes 'supply not scaled to replacement demand' — this quantifies when demand hits.
drivers: FCC firmware cliff Jan 2027 forces simultaneous replacement of ~18K DJI public safety drones
NDAA-compliant DFR antennas not commodity items — require FAA/FCC certification per airframe
Lead time for custom antenna integration: 12-16 weeks per platform

event: FPV component lead times extend 4-6 weeks as Hezbollah FPV surge and Ukraine 10K+/month production compound demand on shared Chinese ESC/motor supply base
evidence_basis: TWZ reports Hezbollah ramping up FPV drone attacks on IDF in Lebanon (April 10), creating new demand vector on same Chinese ESC/motor supply chain serving Ukraine (10K+/month per existing flags) and US Drone Dominance (200K target). Parts DB shows 119/167 ESCs are China-origin, 236/377 frames China-origin. No new Western ESC capacity announced.
probability_basis: Strong inference from confirmed third theater (Lebanon) entering FPV production at scale using identical supply base already strained by Ukraine/US demand. 71% of ESCs in DB are Chinese-manufactured.
drivers: Hezbollah FPV production ramp confirmed by TWZ April 10
Ukraine 10K+/month and US Drone Dominance 200K target already stressing Chinese ESC/motor suppliers
No alternative ESC manufacturing at scale outside China

event: Counter-UAS interceptor demand creates secondary shortage in flight controllers and AI edge processors as Navy, Army, and Israel deploy Coyote-class systems
evidence_basis: TWZ April 10: USS Carl M. Levin shows new launcher (likely counter-drone), USS Bainbridge and USS Winston S. Churchill already carry Coyote interceptors. Bahrain F-16 Block 70s scored drone kills. Pentagon/FAA sign laser C-UAS agreement for Mexico border. Pattern: C-UAS systems use same STM32 flight controllers (47/328 in DB) and companion computers as Blue UAS platforms, but procurement is incremental and outside coordinated supply planning.
probability_basis: Three separate C-UAS deployment articles in single day (Navy launchers, border lasers, Bahrain intercepts) indicate accelerating fielding. Validated flag already notes C-UAS compounds UAS supply pressure (p=0.687). This adds Navy surface fleet as new demand vector.
drivers: Navy deploying counter-drone interceptors on at least 3 destroyers (Levin, Bainbridge, Churchill)
Pentagon/DHS laser C-UAS for Mexico border approved by FAA April 10
Each C-UAS interceptor uses same STM32/companion computer supply as ISR drones

event: LiPo battery cell allocation tightens as C-UAS, border security, and FPV production all spike simultaneously — CATL/BYD prioritize EV over drone cells
evidence_basis: Parts DB shows 221 battery entries, USA 102, China 100 — meaning nearly 45% are Chinese cells repackaged by US integrators. Existing flag notes 'Nearly all drone LiPo packs use Chinese-manufactured cells.' New demand: Pentagon/DHS border C-UAS (laser systems need backup power), Navy destroyer C-UAS interceptors, Hezbollah FPV ramp, Ukraine 10K/month. CATL/BYD/EVE supply 80%+ of global lithium cells and prioritize automotive contracts.
probability_basis: Strong inference from simultaneous demand growth in three sectors (border, naval, FPV) plus known Chinese cell dominance. Medium confidence because battery allocation typically lags component shortages by 1-2 quarters as OEMs burn inventory.
drivers: C-UAS deployments (Navy, border) require high-discharge LiPo packs
Hezbollah FPV ramp adds unforecasted demand on same Chinese cell supply
CATL/BYD prioritize EV contracts over small-format drone cells

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Battery and ESC both bottleneck together because FPV integrators order matched sets — shortage in one cascades to waste in the other

event: European co-production agreements with Ukraine shift 15-20% of FPV component demand to EU suppliers by Q1 2027, partially relieving Chinese supply dependence
evidence_basis: DefenseNews April 10: 24+ Ukrainian defense companies in Paris seeking co-production deals with French partners. Germany already has 11 joint ventures, Spain 5, France just 1. Ukrainian Council of Defence Industry CEO Fedirko confirms intent to integrate with European defense-industrial base. This represents first credible diversification of FPV supply chain away from China-origin motors/ESCs.
probability_basis: Credible signal from primary source (Ukrainian industry CEO at official briefing), but execution risk remains high. Germany's 11 JVs show proof of concept. 15-20% estimate assumes 3-5 French JVs by end 2026 producing motors/ESCs at 2K-5K units/month.
drivers: Ukraine seeking European co-production explicitly to reduce supply chain vulnerability
Germany 11 JVs, Spain 5 JVs operational — France adding capacity
EU regulatory environment favors intra-EU defense production over Chinese imports
